The study involved a large number of students from various schools. The key point here is that the study was supervised by the University of Michigan, but the findings were reported by the Department of Health and Human Services. So option C says the study was carried out by the Department of Health and Human Services. Wait, but the passage says the Department reported the findings, not that they carried out the study. The actual study was done by the University of Michigan. So maybe option C is incorrect. Looking at option A: "America has done a lot to help the teenagers keep off the drugs." The passage mentions that health officials are providing scientific information to reduce drug use, but does it say they've done a lot? It might be implied, but the question is about what we can learn directly from the passage. The passage doesn't go into detail about the efforts made, just the results.
